question_title: What will be the value of "CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index" on 2026-03-12?
summary: The CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index (GVZCLS) is in an elevated regime since early February 2026, with an Ornstein-Uhlenbeck equilibrium around 33.2. The last observation of 33.07 is right at the regime center, implying minimal directional drift. I modeled this using a log-space OU process estimated from the current regime (kappa=0.235, half-life ~3 days), combined with a scenario mixture: 70% probability of staying in the current regime, 15% of a new vol spike (e.g., tariff escalation pushing toward 40+), and 15% of vol declining back to pre-January levels (~26). Gold prices remain near record highs (~$5,350) with strong bullish consensus, and the VIX is elevated at ~20, both supporting continued elevated gold vol. The distribution is right-skewed, reflecting asymmetric tail risk in volatility indices.

factors.description: Current regime stable since Feb 2 with mean ~34.3 and std ~3.2. Last value 33.07 is near regime center, implying stability.
Strong mean-reversion in GVZCLS (OU half-life ~3 days). Current value near equilibrium of 33.2, so little directional drift expected over 10 trading days.
Gold prices surging (~$5,350, up 2% on last day). Continued gold bull run with geopolitical uncertainty supports elevated implied vol.
VIX elevated at ~20 with 6.6% spike on Feb 27. Broader risk-off sentiment could spill into gold vol.
Tail risk: tariff escalation or geopolitical shock could cause another vol spike like Jan 28-29 episode (hit 46).
factors.conditional: Major trade war escalation or geopolitical shock

factors.description: Tail risk: trade resolution or risk-on shift could push vol back toward pre-January regime (~22-25 range).
factors.conditional: Trade tensions resolve or significant market calming

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**CBOE Gold ETF Volatility Index (GVZCLS) on 2026-03-12**

| Percentile | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| P10 | 27.7 |
| P20 | 29.8 |
| P40 | 32.2 |
| P60 | 34.2 |
| P80 | 36.8 |
| P90 | 39.3 |

**Median estimate: ~33.2** (essentially unchanged from the last observation of 33.07)

**Key reasoning:**
- The index is in an elevated regime (mean ~34.3) since early Feb 2026, driven by trade war volatility and gold market turbulence
- Current value (33.07) sits right at the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck equilibrium (~33.2), implying minimal directional drift
- Mean-reversion is fast (half-life ~3 days), so deviations correct quickly
- Distribution is right-skewed to account for asymmetric spike risk (15% scenario weight for another vol spike toward 40+, vs 15% for a decline toward ~26)
- Gold prices near record highs ($5,350) and elevated VIX (~20) support continued high gold implied volatility
